results.title: VIGIL: A Reflective Runtime for Self-Healing LLM Agents
results.snippet: VIGIL (Verifiable Inspection and Guarded Iterative Learning) is a reflective runtime that supervises a sibling agent and performs autonomous maintenance. VIGIL persists as an external, introspective layer—detecting latent faults, identifying structural breakdowns, and proposing concrete fixes to prompt and code.

raw_responses.query: Roman concrete self-healing pozzolanic AI machine learning analogy

results.title: Riddle solved: Why was Roman concrete so durable? | MIT News
results.snippet: Lime clasts in Roman concrete; pozzolanic material from Pozzuoli. Hot mixing creates lime clasts with brittle nanoparticulate architecture that travel through cracks; react with water to create calcium-saturated solution that recrystallizes as calcium carbonate or reacts with pozzolanic material to heal cracks.

results.title: Prediction of crack repair percentage in self-healing concrete using machine learning
results.snippet: Machine learning models predicting crack repair percentage in self-healing concrete.

results.title: Prediction of Healing Performance of Autogenous Healing Concrete Using Machine Learning
results.snippet: ML algorithms predict healing performance of self-healing concrete; pozzolanic reaction between mineral additions stimulates hydration to form C-S-H healing cracks.

results.snippet: AI-driven framework for nano-bio self-healing concrete with nano biomass silica and bacterial admixture.

results.title: Roman concrete - Wikipedia
results.snippet: Roman concrete (opus caementicium) used pozzolanic material and lime. Self-healing properties via lime-clast / pozzolanic reaction creating calcium-aluminum-silicate-hydrate.

results.title: Hot mixing: Mechanistic insights into the durability of ancient Roman concrete
results.snippet: Hot mixing mechanism produces lime clasts giving Roman concrete its self-healing property.
